examples.title: Pseudocode: Graph Representations (Matrix and List)
examples.description: Demonstrates the algorithm for creating and manipulating graphs using both adjacency matrix and adjacency list representations
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CreateAdjacencyMatrix(n)
INPUT: n - number of vertices
OUTPUT: 2D array representing empty adjacency matrix

BEGIN
 CREATE matrix[0..n-1][0..n-1]
 FOR i FROM 0 TO n-1 DO
 FOR j FROM 0 TO n-1 DO
 matrix[i][j] = 0
 END FOR
 END FOR
 RETURN matrix
END

ALGORITHM InsertEdgeMatrix(matrix, u, v, isUndirected)
INPUT: matrix, u, v - vertices, isUndirected - boolean flag
OUTPUT: Updated adjacency matrix

BEGIN
 matrix[u][v] = 1
 IF isUndirected = TRUE THEN
 matrix[v][u] = 1
 END IF
END

ALGORITHM IsAdjacentMatrix(matrix, u, v)
INPUT: matrix, u, v - vertices
OUTPUT: Boolean indicating if edge exists

BEGIN
 IF matrix[u][v] = 1 THEN
 RETURN TRUE
 ELSE
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
END

ALGORITHM GetNeighborsMatrix(matrix, v)
INPUT: matrix, v - vertex
OUTPUT: List of neighbors

BEGIN
 CREATE empty list neighbors
 FOR i FROM 0 TO length(matrix)-1 DO
 IF matrix[v][i] = 1 THEN
 ADD i TO neighbors
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN neighbors
END

ALGORITHM DegreeMatrix(matrix, v)
INPUT: matrix, v - vertex
OUTPUT: Degree of vertex v

BEGIN
 deg = 0
 FOR i FROM 0 TO length(matrix)-1 DO
 IF matrix[v][i] = 1 THEN
 deg = deg + 1
 END IF
 END FOR
 RETURN deg
END

ALGORITHM CreateAdjacencyList(n)
INPUT: n - number of vertices
OUTPUT: Array of n empty linked lists

BEGIN
 CREATE array adjList[0..n-1]
 FOR i FROM 0 TO n-1 DO
 adjList[i] = NULL
 END FOR
 RETURN adjList
END

ALGORITHM InsertEdgeList(adjList, u, v, isUndirected)
INPUT: adjList, u, v - vertices, isUndirected - boolean flag
OUTPUT: Updated adjacency list

BEGIN
 // Add v to u's list (insert at head for O(1))
 newNode = CREATE Node
 newNode.vertex = v
 newNode.next = adjList[u]
 adjList[u] = newNode

 // If undirected, add u to v's list
 IF isUndirected = TRUE THEN
 newNode = CREATE Node
 newNode.vertex = u
 newNode.next = adjList[v]
 adjList[v] = newNode
 END IF
END

ALGORITHM GetNeighborsList(adjList, v)
INPUT: adjList, v - vertex
OUTPUT: List of neighbors

BEGIN
 CREATE empty list neighbors
 temp = adjList[v]
 WHILE temp != NULL DO
 ADD temp.vertex TO neighbors
 temp = temp.next
 END WHILE
 RETURN neighbors
END

ALGORITHM DegreeList(adjList, v)
INPUT: adjList, v - vertex
OUTPUT: Degree of vertex v

BEGIN
 deg = 0
 temp = adjList[v]
 WHILE temp != NULL DO
 deg = deg + 1
 temp = temp.next
 END WHILE
 RETURN deg
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the fundamental algorithms for graph representations. The matrix representation uses O(1) time for edge operations but O(V^2) space. The list representation uses O(V+E) space but requires traversal for edge lookup. The degree calculation differs: matrix requires scanning a row (O(V)), while list only traverses the linked list (O(degree)).

examples.title: Python: Adjacency Matrix Implementation
examples.description: Complete implementation of graph representation using adjacency matrix in Python with weighted and unweighted support
examples.code:
class AdjacencyMatrix:
 """
 Graph representation using adjacency matrix.
 Supports both directed and undirected, weighted and unweighted graphs.
 """
 
 def __init__(self, num_vertices, directed=False, weighted=False):
 """
 Initialize the adjacency matrix.
 
 Args:
 num_vertices: Number of vertices in the graph
 directed: True for directed graph, False for undirected
 weighted: True for weighted graph, False otherwise
 """
 self.num_vertices = num_vertices
 self.directed = directed
 self.weighted = weighted
 self INF = float('inf')
 
 # Initialize matrix with 0 (unweighted) or INF (weighted)
 if weighted:
 self.matrix = [[self.INF for _ in range(num_vertices)] 
 for _ in range(num_vertices)]
 else:
 self.matrix = [[0 for _ in range(num_vertices)] 
 for _ in range(num_vertices)]
 
 # Set diagonal to 0 (no self-loops by default)
 for i in range(num_vertices):
 self.matrix[i][i] = 0
 
 def add_edge(self, u, v, weight=1):
 """
 Add an edge between vertices u and v.
 
 Args:
 u: Source vertex
 v: Destination vertex
 weight: Weight of the edge (default=1)
 """
 if self.weighted:
 self.matrix[u][v] = weight
 else:
 self.matrix[u][v] = 1
 
 # For undirected graph, add reverse edge
 if not self.directed:
 if self.weighted:
 self.matrix[v][u] = weight
 else:
 self.matrix[v][u] = 1
 
 def remove_edge(self, u, v):
 """Remove edge between vertices u and v."""
 if self.weighted:
 self.matrix[u][v] = self.INF
 self.matrix[v][u] = self.INF
 else:
 self.matrix[u][v] = 0
 self.matrix[v][u] = 0
 
 def has_edge(self, u, v):
 """Check if edge exists between u and v."""
 if self.weighted:
 return self.matrix[u][v] != self.INF
 return self.matrix[u][v] == 1
 
 def get_edge_weight(self, u, v):
 """Get weight of edge between u and v."""
 if self.has_edge(u, v):
 return self.matrix[u][v]
 return self.INF
 
 def get_neighbors(self, v):
 """Get all neighbors of vertex v."""
 neighbors = []
 for i in range(self.num_vertices):
 if self.has_edge(v, i):
 neighbors.append(i)
 return neighbors
 
 def get_degree(self, v):
 """Get degree of vertex v (out-degree for directed)."""
 degree = 0
 for i in range(self.num_vertices):
 if self.has_edge(v, i):
 degree += 1
 return degree
 
 def in_degree(self, v):
 """Get in-degree of vertex v (for directed graphs)."""
 if not self.directed:
 return self.get_degree(v)
 
 indegree = 0
 for i in range(self.num_vertices):
 if self.has_edge(i, v):
 indegree += 1
 return indegree
 
 def display(self):
 """Print the adjacency matrix."""
 print("\nAdjacency Matrix:")
 print(" ", end="")
 for i in range(self.num_vertices):
 print(f"{i:3}", end=" ")
 print()
 
 for i in range(self.num_vertices):
 print(f"{i:3}: ", end="")
 for j in range(self.num_vertices):
 if self.weighted and self.matrix[i][j] == self.INF:
 print(" INF", end=" ")
 else:
 print(f"{self.matrix[i][j]:3}", end=" ")
 print()


# Example usage
if __name__ == "__main__":
 # Create undirected graph with 4 vertices
 print("=== Undirected Graph ===")
 g = AdjacencyMatrix(4, directed=False)
 g.add_edge(0, 1)
 g.add_edge(0, 2)
 g.add_edge(1, 2)
 g.add_edge(1, 3)
 g.add_edge(2, 3)
 g.display()
 
 print(f"\nIs edge (0,2) present? {g.has_edge(0, 2)}")
 print(f"Neighbors of vertex 1: {g.get_neighbors(1)}")
 print(f"Degree of vertex 1: {g.get_degree(1)}")
 
 # Create weighted directed graph
 print("\n=== Weighted Directed Graph ===")
 wg = AdjacencyMatrix(4, directed=True, weighted=True)
 wg.add_edge(0, 1, 5)
 wg.add_edge(0, 2, 3)
 wg.add_edge(1, 2, 2)
 wg.add_edge(1, 3, 7)
 wg.add_edge(2, 3, 4)
 wg.display()
 
 print(f"\nWeight of edge (0,1): {wg.get_edge_weight(0, 1)}")
 print(f"Out-degree of vertex 1: {wg.get_degree(1)}")
 print(f"In-degree of vertex 3: {wg.in_degree(3)}")
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates the adjacency matrix representation with full support for directed/undirected and weighted/unweighted graphs. Key operations include O(1) edge lookup, O(V) neighbor traversal, and O(V^2) space complexity. The matrix automatically handles symmetry for undirected graphs.
examples.timeComplexity: Edge lookup O(1), Add/Remove edge O(1), Get neighbors O(V), Get degree O(V), Space O(V^2)

examples.title: Python: Adjacency List Implementation
examples.description: Complete implementation of graph representation using adjacency list with weighted support
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates the adjacency list representation with support for directed/undirected and weighted/unweighted graphs. Uses Python lists instead of linked lists for simplicity. Key operations include O(degree) edge lookup and neighbor traversal, O(1) edge insertion at list head, and O(V+E) space complexity.
examples.timeComplexity: Edge lookup O(degree), Add edge O(1), Get neighbors O(degree), Get degree O(1), Space O(V+E)

examples.title: C: Adjacency Matrix Implementation
examples.description: Complete C implementation of graph representation using adjacency matrix with weighted support
examples.explanation: This C implementation provides a complete adjacency matrix representation with support for both directed and undirected, weighted and unweighted graphs. The implementation demonstrates O(1) edge operations, O(V) neighbor traversal, and includes functions for checking matrix symmetry. Memory is managed dynamically with malloc.
examples.timeComplexity: Edge lookup O(1), Add/Remove edge O(1), Get neighbors O(V), Get degree O(V), Space O(V^2)

examples.title: Java: Adjacency List Implementation
examples.description: Complete Java implementation of graph representation using adjacency list with weighted support and OOP design
examples.explanation: This Java implementation demonstrates the adjacency list representation using Java Collections framework. It features a clean OOP design with an inner Edge class for weighted edges, generic List implementations, and built-in traversal algorithms (BFS and DFS). The implementation handles both directed and undirected graphs with weighted edge support.
examples.timeComplexity: Edge lookup O(degree), Add edge O(1), Get neighbors O(degree), Get degree O(1), Space O(V+E)
